title: config::proxy default_host/default_target return new String per call instead of static
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/config/proxy.rs:27-33
## Violation
```rust
pub fn default_host() -> String { "127.0.0.1".to_string() }
pub fn default_target() -> String { "anthropic".to_string() }
```

These are called by serde's `#[serde(default = "default_host")]` machinery on every deserialization AND by `ProxyConfig::default()`. Each call allocates a fresh String. There is no reason for this — `&'static str` is what these are. The pattern exists because serde's `default` callback signature requires returning an owned value, but the cost can be moved to a const where the data lives once for the program lifetime.

The same pattern repeats across `config/session.rs`, `config/keybindings.rs`, and elsewhere. It's a project-wide micro-pattern that costs a few hundred allocations per process start.
## Mandated refactor
Where serde requires owned, use `const HOST_DEFAULT: &str = "127.0.0.1";` and a thin wrapper `fn default_host() -> String { HOST_DEFAULT.to_string() }` — at least the literal lives once and search-and-replace becomes a one-line change. Better: introduce a `Cow<'static, str>` field type so the default doesn't allocate.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
